query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "citevqa: benchmarking evidence attribution for trustworthy document in" linguistics
rationale: The paper focuses on general human-like attributes (emotional intelligence, conversational coherence) and does not measure the specific mechanism of "evidence attribution" or "document grounding" central to the user's question about CiteVQA. It fails to satisfy any inclusion criteria (a-f) as it addresses a distinct construct (anthropomorphism vs. citation accuracy) within the same broad domain of LLMs.
